HomePage.liveBadgeText: EPUB to PDF, Kindle, TXT, and Word
HomePage.description: Upload an EPUB, choose the reading format, and get a private conversion flow built for books.

pdf.title: EPUB to PDF
pdf.description: Create printable PDFs with book structure intact.

kindle.title: EPUB to Kindle
kindle.description: Convert to AZW3 for Kindle-friendly reading.

markdown.title: EPUB to Markdown
markdown.description: Prepare clean text for notes and AI workflows.

txt.title: EPUB to TXT
txt.description: Extract simple plain text from ebooks.

word.title: EPUB to Word
word.description: Turn ebooks into editable DOCX documents.

readingGoals.title: Start from what you want to do with the book.
readingGoals.description: Each route keeps the same private upload flow, then uses the output format that best fits your next step.

kindle.title: Read on Kindle
kindle.description: Use AZW3 when you want better Kindle compatibility than a generic PDF export.

pdf.title: Print or share
pdf.description: Use PDF when the layout needs to stay predictable across devices.

markdown.title: Use with AI notes
markdown.description: Markdown is planned for structured study notes, summaries, and downstream AI workflows.

docx.title: Edit in Word
docx.description: Use DOCX when you need to revise, comment, or repurpose ebook content.

formatGuide.title: Not sure which format to choose?
formatGuide.description: Pick the output based on the device, workflow, and how much editing you need after conversion.

kindle.label: Kindle reading
kindle.detail: Best first choice for modern Kindle devices.

pdf.label: Printing and sharing
pdf.detail: Stable layout when page appearance matters.

notes.label: Notes and AI workflows
notes.detail: Best for structured text once the workflow is ready.

docx.label: Editing
docx.detail: Use when you need a document you can revise.

comparison.description: Generic converters often hide format tradeoffs. EPUBFlow keeps the reading goal, privacy promise, and download state visible.

storage.question: How long are files stored?
storage.answer: Uploaded and converted files are automatically deleted after 1 hour.

training.question: Do you use my ebooks for AI training?
training.answer: No. EPUBFlow does not use uploaded ebooks for AI training.

kindle.question: Which Kindle format should I choose?
kindle.answer: AZW3 is the best default for modern Kindle devices; MOBI is mostly for older workflows.

drm.question: Can EPUBFlow remove DRM?
drm.answer: No. DRM-protected ebooks are not supported.

formats.question: Which formats are live?
formats.answer: PDF, Kindle AZW3, TXT, and DOCX are wired into the conversion flow. Markdown is presented as a planned workflow.

bottomCta.title: Ready to convert your EPUB?
bottomCta.description: Return to the main upload area, choose a format, and start the private conversion flow.

hero.title: Convert EPUB files for better reading
hero.description: A private ebook conversion workflow for Kindle, PDF, TXT, and Word.

logocloud.title: Built with a modern web stack

integration.title: Integrations
integration.subtitle: Works with your reading workflow
integration.description: Export ebooks into formats that fit devices, documents, and notes.

item-2.title: Kindle
item-2.description: Prepare EPUB files for Kindle reading.

item-3.title: Markdown
item-3.description: Plan structured notes and AI workflows.

integration2.title: Convert once, use anywhere
integration2.description: Choose the ebook output that fits your device or editing flow.

features.title: Features
features.subtitle: Reader-first conversion
features.description: EPUBFlow keeps format choice, privacy, and download status clear.

item-1.title: Private uploads
item-1.description: Files are handled privately and deleted automatically.

item-2.title: Format presets
item-2.description: Choose PDF, Kindle, TXT, or Word based on your goal.

item-3.title: Clear status
item-3.description: Follow upload, conversion, and download states.

item-4.title: Support path
item-4.description: Failed jobs include retry and support actions.

features2.title: Features
features2.subtitle: Built for ebook work
features2.description: A focused conversion flow for readers and researchers.

features3.title: More details
features3.subtitle: Conversion foundations
features3.description: The service is structured so new formats can share one stable workflow.

item-1.title: Upload validation
item-1.description: Rejects invalid or oversized EPUB files early.

item-2.title: Async jobs
item-2.description: Conversion jobs expose stable status polling.

item-3.title: Download proxy
item-3.description: Downloads use the website API for consistent behavior.

item-4.title: Metrics
item-4.description: Core funnel events are tracked by format.

item-5.title: Legal links
item-5.description: Privacy and retention policies stay visible.

item-6.title: Format config
item-6.description: Output labels, extensions, and MIME types are centralized.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Simple pricing
pricing.description: Start with the available converter flow.

item-2.question: Is DRM supported?
item-2.answer: No. DRM-protected ebooks are not supported.

item-3.question: Can I retry a failed job?
item-3.answer: Yes. The converter shows retry and support actions.

item-4.question: What is the refund policy?
item-4.answer: See the refund policy page for details.

item-1.name: Reader
item-1.quote: I can choose the output by reading goal.

item-2.name: Researcher
item-2.quote: Plain text export helps with quick review.

item-3.name: Editor
item-3.quote: DOCX output is useful for editing.

item-4.name: Student
item-4.quote: The format guidance makes the choice easier.

item-5.name: Author
item-5.quote: The conversion status is easy to follow.

item-6.name: Archivist
item-6.quote: Auto-delete is a clear privacy promise.

item-8.name: Reviewer
item-8.quote: TXT is a useful lightweight fallback.

item-11.name: Designer
item-11.quote: The hero keeps the converter visible.

stats.title: Stats
stats.subtitle: Conversion signals
stats.description: Operational signals help keep the converter stable.

calltoaction.title: Start converting
calltoaction.description: Upload an EPUB and choose the output format that fits your reading workflow.

comingSoon.title: Markdown conversion is being shaped carefully.
comingSoon.description: Markdown needs cleaner chapter structure, heading detection, and image handling than a raw text dump. Until that is ready, TXT is the best lightweight export.

reasons.r1: Chapter boundaries should survive the conversion.
reasons.r2: Images and captions need predictable handling.
reasons.r3: The output should work well in note apps and AI tools.

comingSoon.alternativeDescription: Extract readable plain text today while Markdown support is completed.

selector.title: Choose output format
selector.description: Pick the format after your EPUB is attached, then start conversion.

dropzone.title: Drag & drop your EPUB file here

download.title: Download
download.placeholder: Your download link appears here after conversion succeeds.
download.footnote: Files auto-delete after 1 hour. Download promptly. DRM not supported.

privacy.title: Private by default.
privacy.description: Files are encrypted in transit and auto-deleted after conversion. DRM-protected ebooks are not supported.

errors.tooLarge: File is too large. Limit is {max}MB.
errors.missingFile: Please upload an EPUB file first.
errors.timeout: Conversion timed out. Please retry or contact support.
errors.downloadFailed: Download failed. Please retry or contact support.
